changelog:
Two enforcement checkers close silent-failure gaps in the agent tier — the doctrine layer, landing on top of the cycle-2 4.86 base. (1) operating-standard.js: the mission at the top of CLAUDE.md ('resolve it or don't report it') is now ENFORCED through every agent, not merely declared — acting agents must load the anchor skill (verification-before-completion) AND carry an "## Operating Standard" section; report-only agents (disallowedTools: Edit) carry the report-only translation; shadow is exempt. The checker is fleet-safe: it reads each repo's update-config.json operatingStandardSkill, so an adopter that consolidated/renamed the anchor (e.g. Mfa.Hub to dev-process) is never forced to ship a skill it deliberately does not have. Wired into /review:check-templates Step 2c. tools/sync-work.js propagates the doctrine to off-roster adopters (anchor-detect, install checker, patch every agent, verify; dry-run default). (2) skill-resolution.js: a subagent's frontmatter skills list is EAGER full-body injection at startup (Claude Code native — sub-agents.md "Preload skills into subagents"), entering context through the runtime with NO Read event — so a broken or disable-model-invocation skill ref loads nothing SILENTLY (no runtime error, no telemetry signal, the agent behaves as if it had the knowledge). The new checker flags BROKEN_SKILL_REF (no SKILL.md) and UNPRELOADABLE (disable-model-invocation), turning the eyeball-only 'BROKEN SKILL REF' instruction into a tool-detectable check (the F8 primitive). Wired into /review:check-templates Step 2d.

--- v4.86 ---
Cycle-2: build-loop intelligence + pipeline hardening. NEW /route command — a scale-adaptive planning-depth router (the build-loop front door): it classifies a request by Complexity + architectural reach and routes to the right pipeline depth (Tier 0 trivial/bugfix → /do; Tier 1 feature → /todo→/run-todo; Tier 2 module → /create:module; Tier 3 epic/greenfield → /create:new-project or /onboard). Routing != gate-bypass — it only picks an entry point; every downstream hard gate stays in force, skipped phases are named, and the decision is logged. Persisted per-task Complexity score (1-10): /todo + /create:project-epics-todo now emit a Complexity field against one canonical rubric, /do Step 6b routes on it (<=5 Opus-direct, >=6 Sonnet-delegate; missing-field falls back to the signal table; --delegate overrides) and surfaces it at pick-time, /review:optimize-backlog gathers it. Imperative-register softening for Opus 4.8: emphasis-only CRITICAL/MUST across skills+commands softened to plain imperatives while named severity tiers and safety/irreversibility guards are preserved (reduces Opus 4.8 over-triggering). gradeDoc deterministic PRD quality gate (doc-drift.js) wired into /create:project-requirements + /review:check-readiness, plus correctness fixes (annotated all-Must MoSCoW, header-only Success-Criteria table). SHIP_CHECK_OK evidence-gated ship token consumed by /do + /run-todo. /interview now persists its decision summary back into the artifact. memory-lint cross-reference fix (file-path citations no longer mis-read; sentence-final broken refs still caught).

--- v4.85 ---
Skill project-additions marker: skill .md files can now carry a <!-- @@project-additions --> line; everything below it is adopter-owned and preserved under template-updater --merge (the template head above still refreshes), the same way agent Soul Zones survive sync. Closes the hole where any project-specific guidance added to a template skill was clobbered on the next sync — generalizing the one-off skills/brand-guidelines/** project-exception into a section-level mechanism for any skill. No marker (or no --merge) = plain overwrite, exactly as before. Commands that customize template skills (/review:specialize, /sweep, /review:evolve-skills) now write project additions below the marker.
